description_long: This object is a pair of pliers featuring robust, straight metal jaws and red, ergonomically contoured handles. The jaws are flat with a slight taper and include a central notch, likely for gripping or cutting wire. The handles are coated in a red, likely rubberized or plastic material, providing insulation and a comfortable, non-slip grip. The pivot joint is a classic fixed type, allowing the two arms to rotate around a central axis for opening and closing the jaws. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for gripping, bending, or cutting tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A sturdy pair of pliers with red insulated handles and flat, notched metal jaws, designed for gripping or cutting. The handles are ergonomically shaped for comfort and control.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the straight red handles and the flat, notched jaws.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open, curved handles and the central notch in the jaws.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the handle and jaw structure seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the jaws and the separation between the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, insulated, ergonomic handles with a red plastic or rubber coating for comfort and grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, tapered jaws with a central notch for gripping or cutting wire; standard pliers geometry with no needle-nose or diagonal features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed pivot joint allowing standard opening and closing motion; no adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth exterior with a likely rubberized or plasticized texture for enhanced grip; no visible ribbing or knurling.
